message:
Last week my kid's school called during a dead-phone moment—you know, that 2% battery panic spiral—and I missed the whole message. Turns out it was nothing urgent, but sitting there waiting for my phone to charge, I realized I had no idea where I'd written down the school's actual number, or my partner's work contact, or the pediatrician. Everything was *somewhere* in the cloud.

So now we've got a simple printed sheet on the fridge with the people and places that matter most. No battery required. It feels kind of old-school, but honestly? It's made me sleep better.

Do you keep any important contacts or info written down the low-tech way, or is it all digital for you?
